diary:
Woke up in Redmond to that bright early-June sunlight that makes everything feel a little easier. I brewed coffee, glanced at my spreadsheet, and tried to knock out the wedding-client follow-ups before my brain fully woke up. By the time I rolled toward Woodinville, I was already thinking about chair counts and arrival windows—my usual preoccupation.

Work at Woodinville went steady: vendor confirmations, a floor-plan tweak, and a budget reconciliation that always feels like a puzzle box. Around lunch I grabbed something to-go at Willows Lodge and ate outside while double-checking the timeline like it might sprint away if I looked too long.

The day had a weird rhythm—like the light rail closure forced everyone to drive, and it showed in traffic. I stopped by Staples for a few supplies, then wrapped up drafts and a run-of-show back at work.

Later I found vinyl at Ravenna Record Store, then met friends downtown at The 5 Point Cafe for the USA vs Wales match—everything louder, happier. Walking home after, I felt oddly proud of the day: productive, fun, and calm all at once.
thought_process: I planned my morning around being early because weddings punish delays, and today’s spreadsheet-heavy tasks needed quiet focus. Even though the light rail closure only affected a segment of the route, I accounted for the likely slower city flow by leaving with buffer time and keeping errands tight—Staples for essentials, then back to Woodinville to finish quotes and the run-of-show while everything was still in my head. The sunny weather made me more patient at lunch and in traffic; it also nudged me to grab outdoor-friendly snacks for later. The World Cup match mattered more emotionally than logistically—bars opening early and friends gathering downtown gave me a clean “release valve” after work. My mood shifted from careful and task-driven to genuinely relaxed once the game started and the conversations picked up.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
